welcome.title: Let's get started with Grafana Cloud Infrastructure Observability.
welcome.body: Answer just a few questions and Grot can help you get up and running in no time.

I want full visibility into my Kubernetes environment

No, I want to visualize my data without storing it

screens.body:
Browse the full catalog of Grafana Cloud integrations. Each integration includes
prebuilt dashboards, alerts, and guidance for connecting your data.

screens.title: What type of telemetry do you want to store?
What format are your metrics in?
Prometheus
screens.body: Using Prometheus’s remote_write feature, you can send copies of scraped samples to your Grafana Cloud Prometheus metrics service.

links.title: Send metrics to Grafana Cloud using Prometheus remote write

screens.title: Do you want to use the Prometheus InfluxDB_exporter or Telegraf?
InfluxDB_exporter
screens.body: A Prometheus exporter for metrics in the InfluxDB format.

screens.title: Telegraf
screens.body: Point your Telegraf and other services pushing Line protocol metrics to GrafanaCloud via HTTP.

links.title: Push metrics from Influx Telegraf to Prometheus

screens.title: Graphite data ingestion
screens.body: The Grafana Cloud Prometheus metrics service supports receiving Graphite-compatible data.

screens.title: Are you already using OTel Collector?
OTel Collector
screens.body: Grafana Cloud supports receiving telemetry data from the OpenTelemetry Collector.

links.title: Use OTel Collector with Grafana Cloud

screens.title: OpenTelemetry integration
screens.body: This integration helps you set up Grafana Alloy to send all OpenTelemetry data: traces, metrics, and logs, to Grafana Cloud.

screens.title: How do you want to send logs to Grafana Cloud?
HTTP API
screens.body: Send logs to Grafana Cloud using Grafana Loki’s HTTP API by posting JSON payloads representing your log entries. Access your Grafana Cloud account portal to find your hosted endpoint details.

links.title: Send logs using the HTTP API

screens.body: Send logs through OpenTelemetry protocol (OTLP) to Grafana Cloud.

options.text: Infrastructure with Linux and Windows nodes

screens.title: How do you want to deploy?

options.text: With Helm Chart and Grafana Alloy quick deploy
With migrating a Kube-Prometheus Helm stack

screens.title: Grafana Kubernetes Monitoring Helm chart
screens.body: Grafana Kubernetes Monitoring Helm chart is the recommended way to gather your data using Grafana Alloy.

links.title: Configure Kubernetes Monitoring with the Helm chart
Try out Kubernetes Monitoring with Grafana in the Grafana Play GUI

screens.title: What kind of deployment do you have?
Prometheus Operator
screens.body: You can configure Prometheus Operator to scrape an endpoint and send the scraped metrics to Grafana Cloud. Prometheus Operator implements the Kubernetes Operator pattern for managing a Prometheus-based Kubernetes monitoring stack. A Kubernetes Operator consists of Kubernetes custom resources and controller code that abstract away the management and implementation details of running a given service on Kubernetes.

links.title: Try out Kubernetes Monitoring with Grafana in the Grafana Play GUI

screens.title: Helm and Prometheus
screens.body:
The Prometheus Helm chart:
- Installs and bootstraps a one-replica Prometheus Deployment into your Kubernetes Cluster
- Sets up kube-state-metrics, Pushgateway, Alertmanager, and node-exporter
- Configures a default set of Kubernetes observability scraping jobs for Prometheus

links.title: Configure remote_write with Helm and Prometheus
Try out Kubernetes Monitoring with Grafana in the Grafana Play GUI

screens.title: Prometheus ConfigMap
screens.body: You can send scraped samples to Grafana Cloud using the Prometheus remote-write feature if you have Prometheus installed and running in your cluster, and you configured using a Kubernetes ConfigMap.

links.title: Try out Kubernetes Monitoring with Grafana in the Grafana Play GUI

screens.title: Helm and kube-prometheus-stack
screens.body:
Prometheus Operator is a sub-component of the kube-prometheus stack. The kube-prometheus-stack Helm chart installs the kube-prometheus stack, which:
- Configures Prometheus Operator with a default Prometheus-Alertmanager-Grafana stack
- Sets up preconfigured Grafana dashboards and Alertmanager alerts
- Configures a set of Prometheus scrape targets
- Sets up node-exporter and kube-state-metrics

links.title: Configure remote_write with Helm and kube-prometheus-stack
Try out Kubernetes Monitoring with Grafana in the Grafana Play GUI

screens.title: Prometheus Operator with Grafana Cloud
screens.body: You can configure Prometheus Operator to scrape an endpoint and send the scraped metrics to Grafana Cloud. Prometheus Operator implements the Kubernetes Operator pattern for managing a Prometheus-based Kubernetes monitoring stack. A Kubernetes Operator consists of Kubernetes custom resources and controller code that abstract away the management and implementation details of running a given service on Kubernetes.

links.title: Install Prometheus Operator with Grafana Cloud for Kubernetes
Try out Kubernetes Monitoring with Grafana in the Grafana Play GUI

screens.title: Migrating a Kube-Prometheus Helm stack
screens.body: You can set up the Kube-Prometheus stack in your Kubernetes Cluster, then configure it to send its core set of metrics to Grafana Cloud for long-term storage, querying, visualization, and alerting. You can also migrate the stack's core assets (dashboards, recording rules, and alerting rules) to Grafana Cloud. This configuration uses Grafana Cloud's scalability, availability, and efficient performance, as well as reduces load on your local Prometheus instances.

links.title: Migrate a Kube-Prometheus Helm stack to Grafana Cloud
Try out Kubernetes Monitoring with Grafana in the Grafana Play GUI

screens.title: OpenTelemetry Collector
screens.body:
If you currently have an OpenTelemetry Collector-based system in your Cluster, you can:
- Send metrics to Grafana Cloud.
- Enable logs with the OTel Logs Collector.
- Enable the eventhandler integration.

links.title: Send Kubernetes metrics and logs using the OpenTelemetry Collector
Try out Kubernetes Monitoring with Grafana in the Grafana Play GUI

screens.title: Helm and Terraform
screens.body:
You can use Helm and Terraform to configure Kubernetes Monitoring, and use:
- The Kubernetes Monitoring GUI
- Preconfigured dashboards, alerts, and recording rules

links.title: Send Kubernetes metrics, logs, and events to Grafana Cloud with Helm and Terraform
Try out Kubernetes Monitoring with Grafana in the Grafana Play GUI

screens.title: Helm and Argo CD
screens.body:
You can use Argo CD to configure Kubernetes Monitoring, and use:
- The Kubernetes Monitoring GUI
- Preconfigured dashboards, alerts, and recording rules

links.title: Send Kubernetes metrics, logs, and events to Grafana Cloud with Helm and Argo CD
Try out Kubernetes Monitoring with Grafana in the Grafana Play GUI

screens.title: AWS EKS Cluster
screens.body: You can configure Kubernetes Monitoring on your EKS Cluster, either using the AWS console or the AWS CLI.

links.title: Configure on an AWS EKS Cluster
Try out Kubernetes Monitoring with Grafana in the Grafana Play GUI

screens.title: Infrastructure with Linux and Windows nodes
screens.body:
Kubernetes Monitoring supports monitoring Windows nodes within an AWS EKS cluster and then sending to Grafana Cloud the following data for each node:
- CPU
- Memory
- Storage

links.title: Configure Windows Exporter to monitor Windows nodes
Try out Kubernetes Monitoring with Grafana in the Grafana Play GUI

screens.title: Scrape and forward application metrics
screens.body: When you have an application that exports metrics on your Kubernetes Cluster, you can scrape and forward these metrics to Grafana Cloud.

links.title: Try out Kubernetes Monitoring with Grafana in the Grafana Play GUI
